kill_fact: No kill. The load-bearing catalyst survives independent primary-source verification and the spread is still wide open. A DEFINITIVE signed agreement (announced 4/30/26) to sell Long Ridge Energy & Power to a MARA Holdings subsidiary for ~$1.52B is confirmed verbatim by BOTH FTAI's and MARA's own press releases: it eliminates $1.16B of Long Ridge debt at close plus ~$300M of net proceeds to repay parent debt, expected to close Q3/2H 2026 subject to regulatory approval. It is live, dated, binding, and NOT yet fired. The spread is NOT closed: live price $4.95 still sits below the $7.935 52-wk high and far below the ~$9 deleveraged-rail event-target, with the 52-wk low / floor at $3.90 confirmed. The PJM independent market monitor's May 29, 2026 FERC filing is a CONDITIONING request (keep Long Ridge output in PJM markets), NOT opposition or a block — a timing/undertaking risk that leaves FIP's fixed $1.52B consideration intact. CORRECTIONS on three load-bearing facts below.
corrections: (1) OUTSIDE DATE UNVERIFIED: the primary press releases state only 'expected to close in Q3 2026' / 'second half of this year' and disclose NO contractual outside/termination date. The dossier's 'contractual outside date of November 30, 2026' is NOT confirmed in any primary source I could read (it would sit in the merger agreement / 8-K exhibit, which was not independently verified). Treat the Nov 30 backstop as plausible-but-unverified, not established fact. (2) BUYER FINANCING IS A LIVE RISK, not 'fully financed': FTAI's own release explicitly flags 'the risks related to MARA's financing of the proposed transactions' as a forward-looking risk. Secondary reporting referenced a ~$785M Barclays loan, but MARA's financing is not stated as committed/contingency-free in the primary doc; the bull's 'signed AND financed' framing overstates certainty. (3) DEAL-DAY REACTION NARRATIVE ERROR (carried from prior pass): the consensus_delta/positioning_washout imply the market punished the deal ('fell ~7.4% on the Q1 print'); the catalyst-day (4/30/26) reaction was strongly POSITIVE. Any 7.4% drop belongs to a separate Q1-EPS session, not the deal announcement. (4) FLOOR CAVEAT STANDS: the $3.90 floor is the EMPIRICAL 52-wk low, NOT a hard deal-break backstop — FIP is the SELLER (no merger-arb target-floor protection) with NEGATIVE book equity (-$303M) and a senior preferred stack ahead of the common, so a deal-break re-exposes a thin, subordinated stub. The required-approval list (FERC change-of-control / HSR / STB) is not itemized in the primary press release, which says only 'customary closing conditions, including regulatory approvals'; FERC is the confirmed gate via the PJM monitor docket.
evidence: FTAI Infrastructure Inc. press release, 2026-04-30 (IR / GlobeNewswire 3284709): definitive agreement to sell Long Ridge to a MARA Holdings subsidiary for ~$1.52B before adjustments; immediately eliminates $1.16B of Long Ridge debt + ~$300M parent debt repayment; expected to close Q3 2026 subject to customary closing conditions including regulatory approvals; explicitly flags 'risks related to MARA's financing of the proposed transactions' (no stated contractual outside date) - https://ir.fipinc.com/news-releases/news-release-details/ftai-infrastructure-inc-announces-agreement-sell-long-ridge

MARA Holdings IR press release, 2026-04-30: confirms agreement to acquire Long Ridge Energy & Power (485 MW CCGT, gas working interests, ~1,600 acres on the Ohio River) for ~$1.52B as part of its digital-infrastructure / data-center power strategy; close Q3 2026 subject to customary regulatory approvals - https://ir.mara.com/news-events/press-releases/detail/1419/mara-advances-its-optimized-digital-infrastructure-strategy-with-agreement-to-acquire-long-ridge-energy-power

Utility Dive, 'PJM monitor urges FERC to condition Mara power plant buy,' filing dated 2026-05-29 (Docket EC26-92): PJM independent market monitor asks FERC to CONDITION (not block) approval on MARA pledging to keep Long Ridge capacity/energy in PJM markets; regulatory clearance is a prerequisite to a 2H-2026 close - https://www.utilitydive.com/news/pjm-market-monitor-mara-data-center-long-ridge/821574/
Live quote 2026-06-26 (FMP): FIP $4.95, 52-week low $3.90, 52-week high $7.935, market cap ~$585M - the spread to the ~$9 deleveraged-rail target is still fully open and the deal is unpriced
